description_long: This bungalow features a prominent front porch with white railings and wooden steps leading up to the entrance, creating a welcoming facade. The structure is topped with a large, overhanging gable roof, with a visible chimney pipe extending from one side. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal beige siding, complemented by a stone or brick foundation skirting the base. The porch is partially enclosed with decorative latticework, and the roof over the porch is a darker shade, providing visual contrast. Windows are present but limited, with the main focus on the open, airy porch area. The overall design is compact and single-storied, typical of early 20th-century American Craftsman bungalows.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide front porch, gable roof, beige siding, and decorative white railings over a stone foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide porch with white railings, wooden steps, and the overhanging roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the beige siding, stone foundation, and the depth of the porch roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain siding and full expanse of the gable roof, with minimal architectural features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the porch's side entrance, latticework, and the continuation of the stone foundation.
